description.value:
Type: Ninjutsu (Suiton)

Rank: 10

Chakra Cost: 8

Learn DC: | Successes: 4

Perform: 13 | Perform DC: 27

Components: C, S, E, F, Mas

Using this technique, the user covers the weapon with the containment seal with water that rotates violently at high speeds. Once created, the nami-kakushi has two uses -for attack, and for defending against a single Daibakufu, a Suishouha, Deishouha, or Bakusuishouha technique.

Attacking: The nami-kakushi, when discharged, deals weapon damage plus 2d8 extra damage, half water, half force not multiplied on a critical hit. The weapon must hit with a successful melee attack in order to discharge in this manner.

Defending: Once activated the nami-kakushi can disrupt any wave technique that is heading towards the user. The user makes a single attack roll at his highest attack bonus, versus the Saving Throw DC of the technique, which on a hit forces an opposed level check (1d20 + character level, mastery counts) if the wave was casted by a creature. On a success, the wave jutsu finishes prematurely at the end of the next round. On a confirmed critical, the wave jutsu immediately ends, with no further effect. If the wave jutsu allows the caster to ride atop (i.e. Bakusuishouha no jutsu) the creature may suffer fall damage. On a fail, the user suffers the full effects of the technique, and must make a Strength check (DC 15) to not immediately drop the weapon used.

This technique may be coupled with a Charge action to hit the wave. It can also be used against non-casted waves, and requires the user to beat a DC of 15. No levelcheck is required to disrupt the wave.

The user suffers a -4 penalty to level checks to overcome Doton¬-based wave jutsu, and a +4 bonus to level check to overcome Katon-based wave jutsu.

Requires: Chakra Control 10 ranks, Ninpou: Chakra Nagashi (5)

Empower: The user can spend 3 point of chakra to increase the water and force damage by 1d8, to a maximum of 4d8 water damage and 4d8 force damage for 9 points of chakra.

The user can spend 2 points of chakra to gain a +1 bonus to the opposed level check against wave-jutsu, to a maximum of +3 for 6 chakra.

Mastery: The second and fourth step of mastery reduce the penalty to level checks against Doton-based wave jutsu by 1.

The fifth step of mastery allows the user to spend a further 3 points of chakra to increase the water and force damage by 1d8, to a new maximum of 5d8 water damage and 5d8 force damage for 12 points of chakra.

system.target: One creature, or one wave technique

target.value: One creature, or one wave technique
